issues: Background image is too bright and colorful — warm sunset palette clashes with the dark, cinematic tone expected for FF8; makes the page feel more like a travel site than a game fanpage
The two-color hero title (white 'FINAL' + 'VIII', gold 'FANTASY') feels inconsistent and slightly cheap — the color split doesn't read as intentional branding
No dark overlay/vignette on the hero background, causing body text and subtitle to fight with the busy landscape image for legibility
Typography lacks a truly custom display font — the bold sans-serif used is competent but generic, missing the editorial game-site feel of the good references
The page only shows the hero section — no character cards, weapons, world sections are visible to evaluate content depth
No film grain or noise texture overlay to add the cinematic immersion expected of this archetype
The gold/yellow CTA button 'EXPLORE NOW' in the nav conflicts with the outlined 'EXPLORE THE WORLD' button style — inconsistent button system
The stat bar at the bottom (RPG · 1999, PLAYSTATION, NOBUO UEMATSU, 70+ HOURS) uses very small muted text that's nearly invisible against the bright background
No neon accent color system — gold is used but inconsistently; no single cohesive accent color anchors the design
The subtitle text paragraph has poor contrast against the mid-bright background — semi-transparency is insufficient
strengths: Full-bleed hero image is genuinely beautiful and atmospheric — high quality AI-generated landscape art
The stacked typographic treatment of 'FINAL / FANTASY / VIII' creates strong vertical hierarchy and large visual impact
Scroll indicator mouse icon at the bottom is a nice polish detail
The tag badge 'SQUARE ENIX · PLAYSTATION 1999' above the title is a refined design touch
Navigation is clean, minimal, and well-spaced with the right amount of items
Two-button CTA layout (filled + outlined) follows good conversion hierarchy
Bottom metadata strip (genre, platform, composer, playtime) adds real game-companion authenticity
Overall layout structure is well-composed and spacious — content doesn't feel cramped
specific_improvements: Add a dark gradient overlay (bg-gradient from black/70 at bottom to transparent at top) over the hero image to improve text legibility and push it toward a darker, more cinematic tone
Replace the warm sunset landscape with a darker, more atmospheric background — moonlit or twilight version — to match FF8's melancholic tone and align with the dark-theme mandate
Unify the hero title to a single color scheme: either all white with a gold accent on 'VIII' only, or use a consistent gold-white gradient across all three lines
Add a film grain SVG noise overlay (opacity 0.04-0.06) as a fixed pseudo-element over the entire hero to add cinematic texture

Import and apply a custom display font — consider a serif-adjacent or editorial condensed typeface (e.g., Cinzel, Cormorant Garamond, or a condensed display font) to differentiate from generic AI output

Establish a single neon/glow accent color — for FF8's romantic fantasy theme, a soft blue-violet (#7B9EFF or similar) would be thematic and consistent; apply it to the active nav indicator, badge dot, and hover states
Increase the bottom stat bar font size to at least 11px and add a subtle dark backdrop strip behind it to ensure legibility
Add a text-shadow or semi-transparent backdrop-blur pill behind the subtitle paragraph to ensure it reads cleanly over the busy background
Make the 'EXPLORE NOW' nav CTA match the outlined button style used in the hero to create a consistent button system, or adopt the filled gold as the universal primary action style
Build out at minimum the character cards section below the fold — without it, the page feels like only 20% complete as a game companion site
